#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "→ Testing basic execution..."
# NOTE: Cobra strips '--' before arg parsing, so 'tbox run image -- cmd' and
# 'tbox run image cmd' are equivalent. The '--' is kept here for readability
# and Docker-style familiarity; it does not affect flag parsing.
output=$(tbox run ./examples/alpine-cli.tgz -- echo hello 2>&1)
[[ "$output" == *"hello"* ]] || { echo "FAIL: expected 'hello'"; exit 1; }

echo "→ Testing bind mount + env..."
mkdir -p ~/tbox_test
echo "test content" > ~/tbox_test/file.txt
output=$(tbox run ./examples/busybox-static.tgz \
  --env KEY=value \
  --bind ~/tbox_test:/data \
  -- sh -c 'echo $KEY && cat /data/file.txt' 2>&1)
[[ "$output" == *"value"* && "$output" == *"test content"* ]] || { echo "FAIL: bind/env"; exit 1; }

echo "→ Testing DNS resolution..."
output=$(tbox run ./examples/alpine-cli.tgz -- sh -c 'curl -s https://example.com | head -1' 2>&1)
[[ "$output" == *"<!doctype html>"* ]] || { echo "FAIL: DNS"; exit 1; }

echo "✓ All basic tests passed"

    "content": "// internal/termux/fs.go\npackage termux\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"path/filepath\"\n)\n\n// AppPrivateDir returns the Termux-sandboxed base directory for tbox data.\n// Guaranteed to be on an ext4/f2fs filesystem that supports flock.\nfunc AppPrivateDir() string {\n\t// Termux home: /data/data/com.termux/files/home\n\t// We store tbox data in a subdirectory\n\thome := os.Getenv(\"HOME\")\n\tif home == \"\" {\n\t\thome = \"/data/data/com.termux/files/home\"\n\t}\n\treturn filepath.Join(home, \".tbox\")\n}\n\n// SupportsHardlinks probes whether dir's filesystem supports hardlinks.\n// Creates and immediately removes two probe files; returns false on any error.\nfunc SupportsHardlinks(dir string) bool {\n\t// Create a temporary probe file\n\tprobe, err := os.CreateTemp(dir, \".tbox_hl_probe_*\")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\tprobePath := probe.Name()\n\n\tif err := probe.Close(); err != nil {\n\t\t// Log but don't fail — probe cleanup is best-effort\n\t\tfmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, \"Warning: failed to close probe file: %v\\n\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer os.Remove(probePath)\n\n\t// Try to create a hardlink to it\n\tlinkPath := probePath + \".link\"\n\tif err := os.Link(probePath, linkPath); err != nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\tdefer os.Remove(linkPath)\n\n\treturn true\n}\n",

    "path": "internal/engine/process.go",
    "content": "// internal/engine/process.go\npackage engine\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"syscall\"\n\n\t\"github.com/tbox-run/tbox/internal/state\"\n)\n\n// processExists checks if a PID is currently alive\nfunc processExists(pid int) bool {\n\treturn syscall.Kill(pid, 0) == nil\n}\n\n// safeKill verifies the process is actually proot before sending signal.\n// TOCTOU note: there is an inherent race between the existence/comm checks\n// and the actual kill(2) call — a process can exit and its PID be recycled\n// between the two. The /proc/comm check reduces (but does NOT eliminate) the\n// risk. On Android this window is acceptably small; a fully race-free solution\n// would require pidfd_open(2) (Linux ≥ 5.3), not yet universally available\n// on Termux-targeted devices.\nfunc safeKill(pid int, sig syscall.Signal) error {\n\t// First: basic existence check\n\tif err := syscall.Kill(pid, 0); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\t// Second: verify comm name (Android /proc is readable for own UID)\n\t// #nosec G304 — /proc is system directory; pid verified by processExists()\n\tdata, err := os.ReadFile(fmt.Sprintf(\"/proc/%d/comm\", pid))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t// SELinux may block read; fall back to PID-only (documented risk)\n\t\treturn syscall.Kill(pid, sig)\n\t}\n\n\tcomm := strings.TrimSpace(string(data))\n\t// Accept proot binary names across architectures\n\tif comm != \"proot\" \u0026\u0026 !strings.HasPrefix(comm, \"proot-\") {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\n\t\t\t\"PID %d is '%s', not proot — refusing to signal (PID recycling?)\",\n\t\t\tpid, comm)\n\t}\n\n\treturn syscall.Kill(pid, sig)\n}\n\n// containerRunning checks if the container's proot process is still alive.\n// Used by ListContainers to display accurate live/dead status.\nfunc containerRunning(cid string) bool {\n\tst, err := state.Read(cid)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\tif st.Status != \"running\" {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\treturn processExists(st.ProotPID)\n}\n",

    "content": "// cmd/tbox/main.go\npackage main\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"os/exec\"\n\t\"os/signal\"\n\t\"syscall\"\n\n\t\"github.com/spf13/cobra\"\n\t\"github.com/tbox-run/tbox/internal/engine\"\n)\n\nfunc main() {\n\t// Preflight: ensure proot is available\n\tif err := preflight(); err != nil {\n\t\tfmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, \"Error: %v\\n\", err)\n\t\tos.Exit(1)\n\t}\n\n\t// Setup signal forwarding BEFORE any subcommand runs\n\tsetupSignalForwarding()\n\n\t// Root command\n\trootCmd := \u0026cobra.Command{\n\t\tUse:   \"tbox\",\n\t\tShort: \"Docker-like CLI for Android/Termux (userspace, no root)\",\n\t}\n\n\t// run command\n\trunCmd := \u0026cobra.Command{\n\t\tUse:   \"run \u003cimage.tgz\u003e \u003ccommand\u003e [args...]\",\n\t\tShort: \"Run a command in a container\",\n\t\tArgs:  cobra.MinimumNArgs(2), // image.tgz + at least one command arg\n\t\tRunE:  runContainer,\n\t}\n\trunCmd.Flags().StringArrayP(\"env\", \"e\", []string{}, \"Set environment variables\")\n\trunCmd.Flags().StringP(\"workdir\", \"w\", \"/\", \"Working directory inside container\")\n\trunCmd.Flags().StringArrayP(\"bind\", \"b\", []string{}, \"Bind mount host:path[:ro]\")\n\trunCmd.Flags().BoolP(\"verbose\", \"v\", false, \"Show proot stderr output\")\n\trootCmd.AddCommand(runCmd)\n\n\t// ps command\n\trootCmd.AddCommand(\u0026cobra.Command{\n\t\tUse:   \"ps\",\n\t\tShort: \"List containers\",\n\t\tRunE:  listContainers,\n\t})\n\n\t// stop command\n\tstopCmd := \u0026cobra.Command{\n\t\tUse:   \"stop \u003ccid\u003e\",\n\t\tShort: \"Stop a running container\",\n\t\tArgs:  cobra.ExactArgs(1),\n\t\tRunE:  stopContainer,\n\t}\n\trootCmd.AddCommand(stopCmd)\n\n\t// logs command\n\tlogsCmd := \u0026cobra.Command{\n\t\tUse:   \"logs [-f] \u003ccid\u003e\",\n\t\tShort: \"Show container logs\",\n\t\tArgs:  cobra.ExactArgs(1),\n\t\tRunE:  showLogs,\n\t}\n\tlogsCmd.Flags().BoolP(\"follow\", \"f\", false, \"Follow log output\")\n\trootCmd.AddCommand(logsCmd)\n\n\t// Execute\n\tif err := rootCmd.Execute(); err != nil {\n\t\tos.Exit(1)\n\t}\n}\n\n// preflight checks that proot is installed and accessible\nfunc preflight() error {\n\tif _, err := exec.LookPath(\"proot\"); err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\n\t\t\t\"proot not found in PATH\\nInstall in Termux: pkg install proot\")\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// setupSignalForwarding forwards SIGINT/SIGTERM to the proot child.\n// Reads the PID from engine.GetCurrentPID() — the same atomic the engine\n// writes — so the signal actually reaches proot (B1 fix).\nfunc setupSignalForwarding() {\n\tsigCh := make(chan os.Signal, 1)\n\tsignal.Notify(sigCh, syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)\n\n\tgo func() {\n\t\tsig := \u003c-sigCh\n\t\tpid := engine.GetCurrentPID()\n\t\tif pid \u003e 0 {\n\t\t\t// Forward signal to proot process\n\t\t\t_ = syscall.Kill(int(pid), sig.(syscall.Signal))\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Exit with standard Ctrl-C code\n\t\tif sig == syscall.SIGINT {\n\t\t\tos.Exit(130)\n\t\t}\n\t\tos.Exit(1)\n\t}()\n}\n\n// runContainer implements 'tbox run'\n\nfunc runContainer(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {\n\t// Parse: image.tgz \u003ccommand\u003e [args...]\n\t// Note: Cobra strips '--', so we don't require it\n\tif len(args) \u003c 2 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"usage: tbox run \u003cimage.tgz\u003e \u003ccommand\u003e [args...]\")\n\t}\n\n\timagePath := args[0]\n\tcontainerCmd := args[1:] // Everything after image path is the command\n\n\t// Extract flags\n\tenvVars, _ := cmd.Flags().GetStringArray(\"env\")\n\tworkdir, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString(\"workdir\")\n\tbinds, _ := cmd.Flags().GetStringArray(\"bind\")\n\tverbose, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool(\"verbose\")\n\n\tcfg := engine.Config{\n\t\tImagePath:  imagePath,\n\t\tEntrypoint: containerCmd,\n\t\tEnv:        envVars,\n\t\tWorkdir:    workdir,\n\t\tBinds:      binds,\n\t\tVerbose:    verbose,\n\t}\n\n\t// Run container (blocking)\n\texitCode, err := engine.RunContainer(cfg)\n\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tif exitCode != 0 {\n\t\tos.Exit(exitCode)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// listContainers implements 'tbox ps'\nfunc listContainers(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {\n\treturn engine.ListContainers()\n}\n\n// stopContainer implements 'tbox stop \u003ccid\u003e'\nfunc stopContainer(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {\n\tcid := args[0]\n\treturn engine.StopContainer(cid)\n}\n\n// showLogs implements 'tbox logs [-f] \u003ccid\u003e'\nfunc showLogs(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {\n\tcid := args[0]\n\tfollow, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool(\"follow\")\n\treturn engine.TailLogs(cid, follow)\n}\n",

  "916f583433d33436205936d578c51750": {
    "path": "internal/platform/android/proot.go",
    "content": "// internal/platform/android/proot.go\npackage android\n\nimport (\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"strconv\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// EnhanceProotArgs adds Android-specific flags to a proot argument list.\n// The caller is responsible for the base \"-r\", \"/dev\", \"/sys\", \"/proc\" binds.\n// This function adds: root emulation (-0), kernel release, DNS resolv.conf bind,\n// and the mandatory guest bind for PROOT_TMP_DIR.\nfunc EnhanceProotArgs(args []string) []string {\n\t// L2: /proc is always required (no internal emulation in Termux proot).\n\t// The caller in run.go now always adds \"-b /proc:/proc\" unconditionally.\n\n\t// P3 fix: Use root emulation (-0) to ensure the loader can extract\n\t// and execute inside the guest namespace without permission issues.\n\targs = append(args, \"-0\")\n\n\t// P3 fix: Kernel release spoofing allows busybox/musl to see a modern\n\t// kernel version even if the host is older.\n\targs = append(args, \"--kernel-release=6.17.0-PRoot-Distro\")\n\n\t// CRITICAL: Termux proot extracts a helper loader binary into its default PROOT_TMP_DIR ($PREFIX/tmp),\n\t// which is then executed *inside* the guest namespace during execve interception.\n\t// Therefore, this exact directory MUST be bound to the exact same path inside the guest.\n\tprefixTmp := \"/data/data/com.termux/files/usr/tmp\"\n\targs = append(args, \"-b\", prefixTmp+\":\"+prefixTmp)\n\n\t// Bind DNS resolver from Android's actual location\n\targs = AddDNSBind(args)\n\n\treturn args\n}\n\n// GetProotEnv builds the environment variables for proot.\n// CRITICAL P3 fix: Unsets LD_PRELOAD to prevent libtermux-exec.so from\n// interfering with proot's execve interception.\nfunc GetProotEnv(baseEnv []string) []string {\n\tvar env []string\n\tprefixTmp := \"/data/data/com.termux/files/usr/tmp\"\n\n\thasPath := false\n\t// Preserve user env except LD_PRELOAD\n\tfor _, e := range baseEnv {\n\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(e, \"LD_PRELOAD=\") {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(e, \"PATH=\") {\n\t\t\thasPath = true\n\t\t}\n\t\tenv = append(env, e)\n\t}\n\n\t// P3 fix: If no PATH is provided, set a sane default for the guest.\n\t// Otherwise, it might inherit the host's Termux PATH and fail to find\n\t// binaries in /bin or /usr/bin.\n\tif !hasPath {\n\t\tenv = append(env, \"PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Ensure proot uses the standard Termux tmp path for extraction\n\tenv = append(env, \"PROOT_TMP_DIR=\"+prefixTmp)\n\n\treturn env\n}\n\n// AddDNSBind appends the resolv.conf bind mount for Android\nfunc AddDNSBind(args []string) []string {\n\t// Android stores resolv.conf at /system/etc/resolv.conf\n\tresolvers := []string{\n\t\t\"/system/etc/resolv.conf\",\n\t\t\"/data/data/com.termux/files/usr/etc/resolv.conf\", // Termux-specific\n\t\t\"/etc/resolv.conf\",                                // Fallback for non-Android Linux\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, resolver := range resolvers {\n\t\tif _, err := os.Stat(resolver); err == nil {\n\t\t\treturn append(args, \"-b\", resolver+\":/etc/resolv.conf:ro\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// If none found, warn but don't fail — container may have its own resolver\n\treturn args\n}\n\n// GetAPILevel reads ro.build.version.sdk from /system/build.prop.\n// Returns 30 as safe default if unreadable.\nfunc GetAPILevel() int {\n\tdata, err := os.ReadFile(\"/system/build.prop\")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn 30 // safe default for modern Android\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, line := range strings.Split(string(data), \"\\n\") {\n\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(line, \"ro.build.version.sdk=\") {\n\t\t\tparts := strings.SplitN(line, \"=\", 2)\n\t\t\tif len(parts) == 2 {\n\t\t\t\tlevel, err := strconv.Atoi(strings.TrimSpace(parts[1]))\n\t\t\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn level\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn 30\n}\n",

    "path": "internal/logs/tail.go",
    "content": "// internal/logs/tail.go\npackage logs\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"io\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"path/filepath\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/tbox-run/tbox/internal/termux\"\n)\n\n// OpenLog opens (or creates) a log file for a container.\n// Returns (*os.File, error) — callers must check error before use.\nfunc OpenLog(cid, name string) (*os.File, error) {\n\tlogDir := filepath.Join(termux.AppPrivateDir(), \"containers\", cid, \"logs\")\n\tif err := os.MkdirAll(logDir, 0755);     "content": "// internal/state/lock.go\npackage state\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"path/filepath\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/gofrs/flock\"\n)\n\n// WithStateLock acquires flock on \u003ccid\u003e/state.lock, runs fn, releases.\n// Times out after 5 seconds; returns error if lock not acquired.\nfunc WithStateLock(cid string, fn func() error) error {\n\tlockPath := filepath.Join(containerDir(cid), \"state.lock\")\n\tfileLock := flock.New(lockPath)\n\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)\n\tdefer cancel()\n\n\tlocked, err := fileLock.TryLockContext(ctx, 100*time.Millisecond)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"lock error: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tif !locked {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"could not acquire state lock for %s (timeout)\", cid)\n\t}\n\tdefer fileLock.Unlock()\n\n\treturn fn()\n}\n",

    "content": "// internal/state/state.go\npackage state\n\nimport (\n\t\"crypto/rand\"\n\t\"crypto/sha256\"\n\t\"encoding/hex\"\n\t\"encoding/json\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"io\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"path/filepath\"\n\t\"syscall\"\n\t\"time\"\n)\n\n// State represents a container's lifecycle state\ntype State struct {\n\tCID       string    `json:\"cid\"`\n\tImageHash string    `json:\"image_hash\"`\n\tProotPID  int       `json:\"proot_pid\"`\n\tStartedAt time.Time `json:\"started_at\"`\n\tStatus    string    `json:\"status\"` // running, exited, stopped\n\tExitCode  int       `json:\"exit_code,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// Read loads container state from disk.\n// Returns (State{}, error) — callers MUST check error before using State.\nfunc Read(cid string) (State, error) {\n\tpath := statePath(cid)\n\tdata, err := os.ReadFile(path)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tif os.IsNotExist(err) {\n\t\t\treturn State{}, fmt.Errorf(\"container %s not found\", cid)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn State{}, fmt.Errorf(\"read state %s: %w\", cid, err)\n\t}\n\n\tvar st State\n\tif err := json.Unmarshal(data, \u0026st); err != nil {\n\t\treturn State{}, fmt.Errorf(\"corrupt state %s: %w\", cid, err)\n\t}\n\treturn st, nil\n}\n\n// ReadWithHeal loads state and auto-heals stale 'running' if PID is gone\nfunc ReadWithHeal(cid string) (State, error) {\n\tst, err := Read(cid)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn State{}, err\n\t}\n\n\t// Auto-heal: if status is 'running' but PID is dead, mark as crashed\n\tif st.Status == \"running\" \u0026\u0026 !processExists(st.ProotPID) {\n\t\tst.Status = \"exited\"\n\t\tst.ExitCode = -1 // crashed / unknown\n\t\t// Best-effort write; ignore error (read-only path may fail)\n\t\t_ = WithStateLock(cid, func() error {\n\t\t\treturn WriteAtomic(cid, st)\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\treturn st, nil\n}\n\n// WriteAtomic persists state atomically via temp+fsync+rename.\n// Caller MUST hold per-container lock (use WithStateLock).\nfunc WriteAtomic(cid string, st State) error {\n\tdir := containerDir(cid)\n\tif err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0700); err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"create state dir: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Write to temp file first\n\ttmp, err := os.CreateTemp(dir, \"state-*.tmp\")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"create temp: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\ttmpPath := tmp.Name()\n\tdefer os.Remove(tmpPath) // cleanup on failure\n\n\t// Marshal with indentation for readability\n\tdata, err := json.MarshalIndent(st, \"\", \"  \")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"marshal state: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif _, err := tmp.Write(data); err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"write temp: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tif err := tmp.Sync(); err != nil { // fsync!\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"fsync temp: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tif err := tmp.Close(); err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"close temp: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Atomic rename — POSIX guarantees this is atomic\n\treturn os.Rename(tmpPath, statePath(cid))\n}\n\n// GenerateCID creates a short, unique container ID from image path + randomness.\n// Uses crypto/rand so IDs are globally unique even for rapid concurrent runs.\nfunc GenerateCID(imagePath string) (string, error) {\n\th := sha256.New()\n\th.Write([]byte(imagePath))\n\th.Write([]byte(time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339Nano)))\n\trndBytes := make([]byte, 8)\n\tif _, err := rand.Read(rndBytes); err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"generate random bytes: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\th.Write(rndBytes)\n\treturn hex.EncodeToString(h.Sum(nil))[:12], nil\n}\n\n// HashFile computes SHA256 of a file (for content-addressable caching)\nfunc HashFile(path string) (string, error) {\n\tf, err := os.Open(path)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", err\n\t}\n\tdefer f.Close()\n\n\th := sha256.New()\n\tif _, err := io.Copy(h, f); err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", err\n\t}\n\treturn hex.EncodeToString(h.Sum(nil)), nil\n}\n\n// statePath returns the path to a container's state.json\nfunc statePath(cid string) string {\n\treturn filepath.Join(containerDir(cid), \"state.json\")\n}\n\n// containerDir returns the directory for a container's state files\nfunc containerDir(cid string) string {\n\t// Inline AppPrivateDir to avoid import cycle with termux package\n\thome := os.Getenv(\"HOME\")\n\tif home == \"\" {\n\t\thome = \"/data/data/com.termux/files/home\"\n\t}\n\treturn filepath.Join(home, \".tbox\", \"containers\", cid)\n}\n\n// processExists checks if a PID is currently alive (duplicated to avoid cycle)\nfunc processExists(pid int) bool {\n\treturn syscall.Kill(pid, 0) == nil\n}\n",

    "content": "// internal/engine/extract.go\npackage engine\n\nimport (\n\t\"archive/tar\"\n\t\"compress/gzip\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"io\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"path/filepath\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// SafeExtract extracts a .tgz to dst, rejecting any Zip Slip paths.\n// Returns error with path if any entry would escape dst.\nfunc SafeExtract(src, dst string) error {\n\tf, err := os.Open(src)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"open archive: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer f.Close()\n\n\tgr, err := gzip.NewReader(f)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"gzip reader: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer gr.Close()\n\n\ttr := tar.NewReader(gr)\n\tabsRoot, err := filepath.Abs(dst)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"resolve target: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tfor {\n\t\thdr, err := tr.Next()\n\t\tif err == io.EOF {\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"read tar entry: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Resolve the final path and ensure it stays inside dst\n\t\ttarget := filepath.Join(absRoot, hdr.Name)\n\t\tclean := filepath.Clean(target)\n\t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(clean, absRoot+string(os.PathSeparator)) \u0026\u0026 clean != absRoot {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"unsafe archive: path escapes container: %s\", hdr.Name)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// SECURITY: Reject absolute symlink targets that point outside\n\t\tif hdr.Typeflag == tar.TypeSymlink {\n\t\t\tlink := hdr.Linkname\n\t\t\tif filepath.IsAbs(link) {\n\t\t\t\t// Resolve symlink target relative to container root\n\t\t\t\tlinkTarget := filepath.Join(absRoot, link)\n\t\t\t\tlinkClean := filepath.Clean(linkTarget)\n\t\t\t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(linkClean, absRoot+string(os.PathSeparator)) \u0026\u0026 linkClean != absRoot {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"unsafe archive: symlink escapes container: %s -\u003e %s\", hdr.Name, link)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// SECURITY S1: Hardlinks use Linkname as a relative path within the\n\t\t// archive. Apply the same escape check as for regular paths.\n\t\tif hdr.Typeflag == tar.TypeLink {\n\t\t\tlinkTarget := filepath.Join(absRoot, hdr.Linkname)\n\t\t\tlinkClean := filepath.Clean(linkTarget)\n\t\t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(linkClean, absRoot+string(os.PathSeparator)) \u0026\u0026 linkClean != absRoot {\n\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"unsafe archive: hardlink escapes container: %s -\u003e %s\", hdr.Name, hdr.Linkname)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif err := extractEntry(tr, hdr, clean, absRoot); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"extract %s: %w\", hdr.Name, err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// extractEntry extracts a single tar entry to the target path.\n// absRoot is the container root used to resolve hardlink targets consistently\n// with the escape checks performed in SafeExtract.\nfunc extractEntry(tr *tar.Reader, hdr *tar.Header, target, absRoot string) error {\n\tswitch hdr.Typeflag {\n\tcase tar.TypeDir:\n\t\t// Mask to 12 permission bits; \u0026 prevents int64→uint32 overflow for\n\t\t// malformed headers. #nosec G115 — masked value always fits in uint32.\n\t\tmode := os.FileMode(hdr.Mode\u00260o7777) | 0111 // #nosec G115\n\t\treturn os.MkdirAll(target, mode)            //nolint:gosec // G301: container dirs need execute bits\n\n\tcase tar.TypeReg:\n\t\tif err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(target), 0755); err != nil { //nolint:gosec // G301: container dirs need 0755 execute bits\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t\t// The gosec G115 warning is a false positive here — hdr.Mode is\n\t\t// already a valid file mode value from the tar header.\n\t\tmode := os.FileMode(hdr.Mode) // #nosec G115\n\t\tf, err := os.OpenFile(target, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, mode)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t\tdefer f.Close()\n\t\t_, err = io.Copy(f, tr)\n\t\treturn err\n\n\tcase tar.TypeSymlink:\n\t\t// Create parent dirs if needed\n\t\tif err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(target), 0755); err != nil { //nolint:gosec // G301: container dirs need 0755 execute bits\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn os.Symlink(hdr.Linkname, target)\n\n\tcase tar.TypeLink:\n\t\t// R1 fix: resolve hardlink target against absRoot, not filepath.Dir(target).\n\t\t// filepath.Dir(target) gives the destination parent, which is a different\n\t\t// base than the escape check above used — causing an inconsistency that\n\t\t// could let a crafted Linkname bypass the check.\n\t\tlinkTarget := filepath.Join(absRoot, hdr.Linkname)\n\t\treturn os.Link(linkTarget, target)\n\n\tdefault:\n\t\t// Skip unsupported types (devices, sockets, etc.) — not needed for CLI tools\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n}\n",

    "content": "// internal/engine/stop.go\npackage engine\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"syscall\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/tbox-run/tbox/internal/state\"\n)\n\n// StopContainer sends SIGTERM then SIGKILL to the proot process.\n// Safe to call concurrently with RunContainer — no deadlock.\nfunc StopContainer(cid string) error {\n\tvar prootPID int\n\n\t// Phase 1: Read PID under lock, then RELEASE immediately\n\tif err := state.WithStateLock(cid, func() error {\n\t\tst, err := state.Read(cid)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t\tif st.Status != \"running\" {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"container %s is not running (status: %s)\", cid, st.Status)\n\t\t}\n\t\tprootPID = st.ProotPID\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tif prootPID == 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"container %s has no recorded proot PID\", cid)\n\t}\n\n\t// Phase 2: Send signals and poll WITHOUT holding any lock\n\t// This prevents deadlock with RunContainer's final state write\n\n\t// SIGTERM first (graceful shutdown)\n\tif err := safeKill(prootPID, syscall.SIGTERM); err != nil {\n\t\tif err == syscall.ESRCH {\n\t\t\t// Already dead — notify user rather than returning silently\n\t\t\tfmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, \"Container %s is already stopped.\\n\", cid)\n\t\t\treturn markStopped(cid)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"send SIGTERM to PID %d: %w\", prootPID, err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Poll for termination (10s timeout, 200ms intervals)\n\tdeadline := time.Now().Add(10 * time.Second)\n\tfor time.Now().Before(deadline) {\n\t\tif !processExists(prootPID) {\n\t\t\treturn markStopped(cid)\n\t\t}\n\t\ttime.Sleep(200 * time.Millisecond)\n\t}\n\n\t// Phase 3: SIGKILL fallback\n\tif err := safeKill(prootPID, syscall.SIGKILL); err != nil \u0026\u0026 err != syscall.ESRCH {\n\t\t// FIX: Use os.Stderr instead of undefined stderrLog\n\t\tfmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, \"Warning: SIGKILL failed: %v\\n\", err)\n\t}\n\ttime.Sleep(500 * time.Millisecond) // Let kernel clean up\n\n\treturn markStopped(cid)\n}\n\n// markStopped updates state to 'stopped' ONLY if not already 'exited'\n// This preserves exit codes from natural termination\nfunc markStopped(cid string) error {\n\treturn state.WithStateLock(cid, func() error {\n\t\tst, err := state.Read(cid)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t\t// CRITICAL: If RunContainer already wrote 'exited', preserve it\n\t\tif st.Status == \"exited\" || st.Status == \"stopped\" {\n\t\t\tfmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, \"Container %s is already stopped (status: %s).\\n\", cid, st.Status)\n\t\t\treturn nil // natural exit wins; don't overwrite exit code\n\t\t}\n\t\tst.Status = \"stopped\"\n\t\treturn state.WriteAtomic(cid, st)\n\t})\n}\n",

falls back to full copy with warning.\n// Returns path to the overlay root (used as proot -r argument).\nfunc PrepareOverlay(imageHash, cid string, cfg Config) (string, error) {\n\tsrc := imagePath(imageHash)\n\tdst := overlayPath(cid)\n\n\t// Create container directory structure\n\tif err := os.MkdirAll(dst, 0755); err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"create overlay dir: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Create /tbox virtual API directory (real directory, not bind mount)\n\ttboxDir := filepath.Join(dst, \"tbox\")\n\tif err := os.MkdirAll(tboxDir, 0755); err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"create /tbox dir: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Populate /tbox with initial structure\n\tfor _, sub := range []string{\"config\", \"secrets\", \"ipc\", \"health\"} {\n\t\tif err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(tboxDir, sub), 0755); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"create /tbox/%s: %w\", sub, err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// CoW strategy: hardlink tree if filesystem supports it\n\tif termux.SupportsHardlinks(dst) \u0026\u0026 prootLink2SymlinkEnabled() {\n\t\treturn dst, hardlinkTree(src, dst)\n\t}\n\n\t// Fallback: full copy (slower, but works on all filesystems)\n\tfmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr,\n\t\t\"Warning: hardlinks or proot --link2symlink unavailable; using full copy (slower)\")\n\treturn dst, copyTree(src, dst)\n}\n\n// hardlinkTree creates a hardlinked copy of src at dst (CoW base)\nfunc hardlinkTree(src, dst string) error {\n\tif err := validatePath(src); err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid src: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tif err := validatePath(dst); err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid dst: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tcmd := exec.Command(\"cp\", \"-al\", src+\"/.\", dst)\n\t// cp -al produces no stdout on success; discard to avoid false-error output.\n\tcmd.Stdout = io.Discard\n\tcmd.Stderr = os.Stderr\n\treturn cmd.Run()\n}\n\nfunc validatePath(p string) error {\n\tclean := filepath.Clean(p)\n\tabs, err := filepath.Abs(clean)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\t// Ensure path is under Termux app-private dir\n\tappDir := termux.AppPrivateDir()\n\tif !strings.HasPrefix(abs, appDir) {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"path %s escapes allowed directory %s\", abs, appDir)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// copyTree creates a full recursive copy of src at dst\nfunc copyTree(src, dst string) error {\n\tif err := validatePath(src); err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid src: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tif err := validatePath(dst); err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid dst: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tcmd := exec.Command(\"cp\", \"-rP\", src+\"/.\", dst)\n\t// cp -rP produces no stdout on success; discard to avoid false-error output.\n\tcmd.Stdout = io.Discard\n\tcmd.Stderr = os.Stderr\n\treturn cmd.Run()\n}\n\n// prootLink2SymlinkEnabled checks if proot's --link2symlink works\n// (may be blocked by SELinux on some Android devices)\nfunc prootLink2SymlinkEnabled() bool {\n\t// Quick test: try proot with --link2symlink on a dummy command.\n\t// We MUST unset LD_PRELOAD here too, otherwise the check itself may fail!\n\tcmd := exec.Command(\"proot\", \"--link2symlink\", \"/data/data/com.termux/files/usr/bin/true\")\n\tcmd.Env = android.GetProotEnv(os.Environ())\n\tout, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tfmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, \"[DEBUG] prootLink2SymlinkEnabled failed! error: %v, output: %s\\n\", err, string(out))\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\treturn true\n}\n\n// imagePath returns the cached image rootfs path\nfunc imagePath(imageHash string) string {\n\treturn filepath.Join(termux.AppPrivateDir(), \"images\", imageHash, \"rootfs\")\n}\n\n// overlayPath returns the container overlay path\nfunc overlayPath(cid string) string {\n\treturn filepath.Join(termux.AppPrivateDir(), \"containers\", cid, \"overlay\")\n}\n",

    "content": "// internal/engine/run.go\npackage engine\n\nimport (\n\t\"bytes\"\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"io\"\n\t\"math\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"os/exec\"\n\t\"path/filepath\"\n\t\"regexp\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"sync/atomic\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/tbox-run/tbox/internal/logs\"\n\t\"github.com/tbox-run/tbox/internal/platform/android\"\n\t\"github.com/tbox-run/tbox/internal/state\"\n\t\"github.com/tbox-run/tbox/internal/termux\"\n)\n\n// globalProotPID holds the PID of the active proot process for signal forwarding.\n// Written atomically by RunContainer; read by GetCurrentPID (called from main).\nvar globalProotPID int32\n\n// GetCurrentPID returns the PID of the currently running proot process, or 0.\nfunc GetCurrentPID() int32 {\n\treturn atomic.LoadInt32(\u0026globalProotPID)\n}\n\n// Config holds container execution parameters\ntype Config struct {\n\tImagePath  string   // Path to .tgz image file\n\tEntrypoint []string // Command to execute inside container\n\tEnv        []string // Environment variables (KEY=VAL)\n\tWorkdir    string   // Working directory inside container\n\tBinds      []string // Bind mounts: host:container[:ro]\n\tVerbose    bool     // Show raw proot stderr\n}\n\n// RunContainer executes cmd inside container using proot.\n// Blocks until the process exits. Returns exit code and any error.\nfunc RunContainer(cfg Config) (int, error) {\n\n\tprootTmp := filepath.Join(termux.AppPrivateDir(), \"tmp\")\n\tif err := os.MkdirAll(prootTmp, 0700); err != nil {\n\t\treturn -1, fmt.Errorf(\"create proot tmp dir: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\t// Generate container ID (content-addressable by image + timestamp)\n\tcid, err := generateCID(cfg.ImagePath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn -1, fmt.Errorf(\"generate CID: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Phase 1: Extract image to cache (content-addressable by tarball hash)\n\timageHash, err := hashFile(cfg.ImagePath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn -1, fmt.Errorf(\"hash image: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\timageRoot := filepath.Join(termux.AppPrivateDir(), \"images\", imageHash, \"rootfs\")\n\n\t// Check cache; extract if missing\n\tif _, err := os.Stat(imageRoot); os.IsNotExist(err) {\n\t\tif err := os.MkdirAll(imageRoot, 0755); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn -1, fmt.Errorf(\"create image dir: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err := SafeExtract(cfg.ImagePath, imageRoot); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn -1, fmt.Errorf(\"extract image: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Phase 2: Prepare CoW overlay for this container\n\toverlayPath, err := PrepareOverlay(imageHash, cid, cfg)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn -1, fmt.Errorf(\"prepare overlay: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Phase 3: Setup log files\n\tstdoutLog, err := logs.OpenLog(cid, \"stdout.log\")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn -1, fmt.Errorf(\"open stdout log: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer stdoutLog.Close()\n\n\tstderrLog, err := logs.OpenLog(cid, \"stderr.log\")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn -1, fmt.Errorf(\"open stderr log: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer stderrLog.Close()\n\n\t// Phase 5: Build and start proot command.\n\t// stderrBuf MUST be created before cmd.Start() — reassigning cmd.Stderr\n\t// after Start() is silently ignored by the OS (B2 fix).\n\tvar stderrBuf bytes.Buffer\n\t// Finalize environment (P3 fix: unset LD_PRELOAD, add guest PATH)\n\tsanitizedEnv := android.GetProotEnv(cfg.Env)\n\n\t// Set PROOT_L2S_DIR if link2symlink is enabled\n\tif prootLink2SymlinkEnabled() {\n\t\tl2sDir := filepath.Join(overlayPath, \".l2s\")\n\t\t_ = os.MkdirAll(l2sDir, 0700)\n\t\tsanitizedEnv = append(sanitizedEnv, \"PROOT_L2S_DIR=\"+l2sDir)\n\t}\n\n\tprootArgs := buildProotArgs(overlayPath, cfg, sanitizedEnv)\n\n\tcmd := exec.Command(\"proot\", prootArgs...)\n\tcmd.Stdout = io.MultiWriter(stdoutLog, os.Stdout)\n\tcmd.Stderr = io.MultiWriter(stderrLog, os.Stderr, \u0026stderrBuf)\n\n\t// Environment for proot process itself\n\tcmd.Env = sanitizedEnv\n\n\t// Create PROOT_TMP_DIR before executing proot\n\ttmpDir := filepath.Join(termux.AppPrivateDir(), \"tmp\")\n\t_ = os.MkdirAll(tmpDir, 0755)\n\n\t// CRITICAL: Termux proot requires the exact loader path to exist inside the guest\n\t// for the bind-mount to succeed. We must create the identical path inside the overlay.\n\toverlayTmpDir := filepath.Join(overlayPath, strings.TrimPrefix(tmpDir, \"/\"))\n\t_ = os.MkdirAll(overlayTmpDir, 0755)\n\n\t// We removed PROOT_TMP_DIR override. Termux proot will default to $PREFIX/tmp.\n\t// We must ensure the overlay has $PREFIX/tmp created so proot can bind to it.\n\tprefixTmp := \"/data/data/com.termux/files/usr/tmp\"\n\toverlayPrefixTmp := filepath.Join(overlayPath, strings.TrimPrefix(prefixTmp, \"/\"))\n\t_ = os.MkdirAll(overlayPrefixTmp, 0755)\n\n\t// P3 fix: Hide SELinux to prevent apps/loaders from getting blocked or confused\n\tselinuxDir := filepath.Join(overlayPath, \"sys/fs/.empty\")\n\t_ = os.MkdirAll(selinuxDir, 0700)\n\n\tfmt.Printf(\"[DEBUG] Executing proot command: proot %s\\n\", strings.Join(prootArgs, \" \"))\n\n\tif err := cmd.Start(); err != nil {\n\t\treturn -1, fmt.Errorf(\"start proot: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Store PID atomically for signal forwarding\n\tpid := cmd.Process.Pid\n\tif pid \u003e math.MaxInt32 || pid \u003c math.MinInt32 {\n\t\treturn -1, fmt.Errorf(\"proot PID %d out of range for atomic storage\", pid)\n\t}\n\tatomic.StoreInt32(\u0026globalProotPID, int32(pid))\n\n\t// Write initial state under lock\n\tif err := state.WithStateLock(cid, func() error {\n\t\treturn state.WriteAtomic(cid, state.State{\n\t\t\tCID:       cid,\n\t\t\tImageHash: imageHash,\n\t\t\tProotPID:  cmd.Process.Pid,\n\t\t\tStatus:    \"running\",\n\t\t\tStartedAt: time.Now(), // L2 fix: must be set or ps shows year 0001\n\t\t})\n\t}); err != nil {\n\t\t// Non-fatal: continue execution, state will be healed on next read\n\t\tfmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, \"Warning: could not write initial state: %v\\n\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Phase 6: BLOCKING wait for completion\n\terr = cmd.Wait()\n\n\t// Determine exit code\n\texitCode := 0\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tvar exitErr *exec.ExitError\n\t\tif errors.As(err, \u0026exitErr) {\n\t\t\texitCode = exitErr.ExitCode()\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\texitCode = -1\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Phase 7: Filter stderr and write final state\n\tstderr := stderrBuf.String()\n\tif exitCode == 0 {\n\t\t// Only filter harmless patterns on success\n\t\tstderr = filterHarmlessPatterns(stderr)\n\t\tif cfg.Verbose \u0026\u0026 stderr != \"\" {\n\t\t\tfmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, \"[proot] %s\", stderr)\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\t// On failure, provide contextual error messages\n\t\tif strings.Contains(stderr, \"ptrace\") || strings.Contains(stderr, \"seccomp\") {\n\t\t\treturn exitCode, fmt.Errorf(\n\t\t\t\t\"proot platform error: %s\\n→ Check: Android API level, SELinux policy, battery optimization\",\n\t\t\t\tstrings.TrimSpace(stderr))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif cfg.Verbose {\n\t\t\tfmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, \"[proot stderr] %s\", stderr)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Write final state under lock\n\tif err := state.WithStateLock(cid, func() error {\n\t\treturn state.WriteAtomic(cid, state.State{\n\t\t\tCID:       cid,\n\t\t\tImageHash: imageHash,\n\t\t\tProotPID:  cmd.Process.Pid,\n\t\t\tStatus:    \"exited\",\n\t\t\tExitCode:  exitCode,\n\t\t})\n\t}); err != nil {\n\t\tfmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, \"Warning: could not write final state: %v\\n\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn exitCode, nil\n}\n\n// buildProotArgs constructs the proot command line\nfunc buildProotArgs(overlayPath string, cfg Config, env []string) []string {\n\t// Base rootfs bind. We omit -0 here as android.EnhanceProotArgs adds it.\n\targs := []string{\"-r\", overlayPath}\n\n\tif prootLink2SymlinkEnabled() {\n\t\targs = append(args, \"--link2symlink\")\n\t}\n\n\t// P3 fix: Hide SELinux\n\tselinuxDir := filepath.Join(overlayPath, \"sys/fs/.empty\")\n\targs = append(args, \"-b\", selinuxDir+\":/sys/fs/selinux\")\n\n\t// System binds (always required by proot)\n\targs = append(args,\n\t\t\"-b\", \"/dev:/dev\",\n\t\t\"-b\", \"/sys:/sys\",\n\t\t// L2 fix: /proc must always be bound. Proot has no internal /proc\n\t\t// emulation — removing this bind breaks containers on all API levels.\n\t\t// The recursion concern is mitigated by PROOT_NO_SECCOMP=1 if needed.\n\t\t\"-b\", \"/proc:/proc\",\n\t)\n\n\t// L1 fix: delegate Android-specific args (resolv.conf, future platform\n\t// quirks) to EnhanceProotArgs — the abstraction layer that was bypassed.\n\targs = android.EnhanceProotArgs(args)\n\n\t// User-specified binds (appended last — user has final say on conflicts)\n\tfor _, bind := range cfg.Binds {\n\t\targs = append(args, \"-b\", bind)\n\t}\n\n\t// Working directory\n\tworkdir := cfg.Workdir\n\tif workdir == \"\" {\n\t\tworkdir = \"/\"\n\t}\n\targs = append(args, \"-w\", workdir)\n\n\t// Entry point command prefixed with env to handle environment variables\n\tif len(env) \u003e 0 {\n\t\targs = append(args, \"/usr/bin/env\", \"-i\")\n\t\targs = append(args, env...)\n\t}\n\targs = append(args, cfg.Entrypoint...)\n\n\treturn args\n}\n\n// harmlessPatterns are compiled once at init time for efficiency.\n// L4 fix: use regexp.MustCompile — strings.ReplaceAll did literal matching,\n// so patterns with `.*` never matched real proot output.\nvar harmlessPatterns = []*regexp.Regexp{\n\tregexp.MustCompile(`(?m)^.*ptrace: Operation not permitted.*$\\n?`),\n\tregexp.MustCompile(`(?m)^.*seccomp: .* not supported.*$\\n?`),\n\tregexp.MustCompile(`(?m)^.*warning: unable to.*$\\n?`),\n}\n\n// filterHarmlessPatterns removes expected proot warnings from stderr\nfunc filterHarmlessPatterns(stderr string) string {\n\tresult := stderr\n\tfor _, re := range harmlessPatterns {\n\t\tresult = re.ReplaceAllString(result, \"\")\n\t}\n\treturn strings.TrimSpace(result)\n}\n\n// generateCID creates a short, unique container ID\nfunc generateCID(imagePath string) (string, error) {\n\t// Simple: hash(imagePath + timestamp + random)\n\t// Phase 1: use first 12 hex chars of SHA256\n\treturn state.GenerateCID(imagePath)\n}\n\n// hashFile computes SHA256 of a file (for content-addressable caching)\nfunc hashFile(path string) (string, error) {\n\treturn state.HashFile(path)\n}\n\n// (globalProotPID and GetCurrentPID are declared at the top of this file)\n\n// ListContainers prints a table of all known containers.\n// Reads state files without acquiring locks (relies on atomic     "content": "#!/bin/bash\nset -e\n\necho \"→ Testing deadlock-free stop...\"\n# Run container in background; capture the tbox process PID separately.\n# CID1 ($!) is the shell job PID — not the container ID.\n# We use `tbox ps` (without -q, which is unimplemented) to find the container.\ntbox run ./examples/alpine-cli.tgz sleep 30 \u0026\nTBOX_PID1=$!\nsleep 2 # Let container start and register its state\n\n# Retrieve the first container ID from tbox ps output (skip header line)\nCONTAINER_ID=$(tbox ps | awk 'NR==2{print $1}')\nif [ -z \"$CONTAINER_ID\" ]; then\n    echo \"✗ No running container found; ps output:\" \u003e\u00262\n    tbox ps \u003e\u00262\n    kill \"$TBOX_PID1\" 2\u003e/dev/null || true\n    exit 1\nfi\necho \"  Stopping container $CONTAINER_ID\"\ntbox stop \"$CONTAINER_ID\"   # Should not hang\nwait \"$TBOX_PID1\" 2\u003e/dev/null || true\n\necho \"→ Testing concurrent runs (ptrace contention)...\"\ntbox run ./examples/alpine-cli.tgz sleep 5 \u0026\nPID1=$!\ntbox run ./examples/busybox-static.tgz sleep 5 \u0026\nPID2=$!\nwait $PID1 $PID2 2\u003e/dev/null || true\n# Either both succeed, or one fails with actionable error — never hang\n\necho \"✓ All concurrent tests passed\"",

c68d55692688a7fb2413f30549f1abe2.content:
# tbox Phase 1 Limitations

These are NOT bugs — they are deliberate scope decisions or platform constraints.

## Platform Constraints (Android)
- **No kernel namespaces**: Container sees host PID tree, network, mounts. Not a sandbox.
- **ptrace overhead**: 3-8% for CLI tools; 20-50% for file-heavy ops; 10-100x for tight syscall loops.
- **SELinux variability**: Some OEM policies block `/proc/<pid>/comm` reads. Fallback to PID-only (documented <1% false-positive risk).
- **Scoped Storage (Android 10+)**: Cannot bind arbitrary `/sdcard` paths. Use `termux-setup-storage` and bind only granted subpaths.

## Phase 1 Scope Decisions
- **Blocking execution only**: `tbox run` does not support `-d`. Use shell `&` for backgrounding (exit codes not captured).
- **No port publishing**: Containers share host network. Bind services to `127.0.0.1`.
- **No orphan cleanup**: If proot is killed, traced processes may linger. Use `tbox ps` + manual `kill` if needed.
- **Tarball-based cache**: Repacking an image creates a new cache entry. Use `tbox image prune` (Phase 2) to manage disk.
- **No log rotation**: `stdout.log` grows unbounded. Monitor disk usage; Phase 2 adds size-based rotation.

## Unsupported Features (Fundamental)
- **No setuid support**: Binaries requiring setuid (`sudo`, `ping`) will fail. Use static alternatives.
- **No device node access**: `/dev/null`, `/dev/zero` emulated; hardware devices (`/dev/sda`) inaccessible.
- **No real isolation**: By design — tbox is for convenience, not security.

# tbox Security Model

> ⚠️ **tbox is NOT a security boundary**.
> Container processes run as the Termux app UID and share the host PID tree, 
> network stack, and mount table. Any host path you explicitly bind is fully 
> accessible to the container. **Do not run untrusted code**.

## Safe Usage ✅
- Run your own trusted CLI tools (curl, python, node, static binaries)
- Bind only non-sensitive host directories (`~/projects`, not `/data/data`)
- Use for prototyping, learning, and lightweight dev/ops tasks

## Unsafe Usage ❌
- Run untrusted or third-party binaries from the internet
- Bind sensitive paths: `/data/data`, `/system`, `/sdcard/Android`
- Assume network or process isolation (container shares host stack)

## Zip Slip Protection
tbox validates every path during rootfs extraction. Archives containing 
`../` path traversal or absolute symlinks pointing outside the container 
are rejected with an error.

## Vulnerability Reports
Report security issues to security@tbox.run with:
- Android version + API level
- Termux package versions (`pkg list-installed`)
- Steps to reproduce the issue

#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "→ Testing deadlock-free stop..."
# Run container in background; capture the tbox process PID separately.
# CID1 ($!) is the shell job PID — not the container ID.
# We use `tbox ps` (without -q, which is unimplemented) to find the container.
tbox run ./examples/alpine-cli.tgz sleep 30 &
TBOX_PID1=$!
sleep 2 # Let container start and register its state

# Retrieve the first container ID from tbox ps output (skip header line)
CONTAINER_ID=$(tbox ps | awk 'NR==2{print $1}')
if [ -z "$CONTAINER_ID" ]; then
    echo "✗ No running container found; ps output:" >&2
    tbox ps >&2
    kill "$TBOX_PID1" 2>/dev/null || true
    exit 1
fi
echo "  Stopping container $CONTAINER_ID"
tbox stop "$CONTAINER_ID"   # Should not hang
wait "$TBOX_PID1" 2>/dev/null || true

echo "→ Testing concurrent runs (ptrace contention)..."
tbox run ./examples/alpine-cli.tgz sleep 5 &
PID1=$!
tbox run ./examples/busybox-static.tgz sleep 5 &
PID2=$!
wait $PID1 $PID2 2>/dev/null || true
# Either both succeed, or one fails with actionable error — never hang

echo "✓ All concurrent tests passed"
